title: Salvia angustiarum
what_description: Salvia angustiarum is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is characterized by its upright growth habit, reaching heights of up to 1 meter, with a spreading crown. The leaves are lanceolate in shape, measuring 5-10 cm in length and 1-2 cm in width, with a glabrous upper surface and a pubescent lower surface. The inflorescence is a terminal spike, bearing purple flowers that are bilabiate and zygomorphic, with a 2-lipped corolla, where the upper lip is 13-15 mm long and the lower lip is 12-15 mm long. The plant has a fibrous root system and a woody base, with a taproot that stores water, allowing it to survive drought conditions.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Salvia angustiarum are anxiety, which has been alleviated in patients through its anxiolytic effects, depression, where its neuroprotective properties have contributed to improved mood, insomnia, as the plant's sedative properties help regulate sleep patterns. The herb has also been used to treat stress, with its adaptogenic properties helping the body adapt to stressful situations, and pain, which has been relieved through its analgesic effects. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating migraines, and its antispasmodic properties have helped alleviate muscle spasms and digestive issues. Salvia angustiarum has also been used to treat headaches, nausea, fatigue, epilepsy, pain relief, digestion, chronic pain, gastrointestinal issues, skin issues, rheumatism, and panic attacks, showcasing its broad range of therapeutic applications.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Salvia angustiarum are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which helps to neutralize free radicals and protect against cell damage. The plant's antimicrobial properties, primarily due to its volatile oils, inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Salvia angustiarum's anti-inflammatory properties are also noteworthy, as it contains flavonoids and saponins that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions. Furthermore, its neuroprotective effects are linked to the modulation of neurotransmitter activity, thereby exerting a protective influence on the nervous system. Additionally, the plant's analgesic properties, derived from its flavonoid and saponin content, provide relief from pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Salvia angustiarum are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, salvianolic acid A, ursolic acid, and salvianolic acid B, which possess significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties due to their ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit enzymes involved in inflammatory pathways, rosmarinic acid and quercetin also exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, salvianolic acid A has been shown to have a strong inhibitory effect on alpha-glucosidase, ursolic acid has demonstrated anti-tumor and anti-inflammatory effects, and salvianolic acid B has been found to have a protective effect on the brain by inhibiting lipid peroxidation and reducing oxidative stress.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Salvia angustiarum are derived from its leaves and flowers, which contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Salvia tea is a common preparation, made by steeping dried leaves in hot water to extract its active compounds. Salvia tincture is a concentrated liquid extract, typically made with a solvent like ethanol, which enhances the bioavailability of its constituents. Salvia infusion is a method of extracting the plant's active compounds using cold water, similar to tea but with a higher water-to-herb ratio. Salvia oil and capsules are also used, the former often obtained through steam distillation of the plant's leaves and flowers, while the latter typically containing a standardized extract of the plant's bioactive compounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Salvia angustiarum improperly include experiencing hallucinations due to its potent psychoactive compound salvinorin A which affects the brain's default mode network and can lead to distorted perceptions of reality. This can also lead to developing anxiety and paranoia as a result of the intense and disorienting experiences that users may encounter. In some cases, users may experience flashbacks to the hallucinatory episode, making it difficult to distinguish reality from fantasy. Furthermore, users who are prone to mental health issues such as depression or schizophrenia may be more susceptible to these negative effects. This is because Salvia angustiarum can exacerbate these conditions by amplifying their underlying symptoms and increasing stress levels.
uses: Salvia angustiarum has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It may help to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and anxiety. Some studies suggest that Salvia angustiarum may also have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Additionally, it may be used to treat digestive issues and menstrual cramps due to its reported ability to relax the muscles. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Salvia angustiarum.
taxonomy: Salvia angustiarum is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its paired leaves and inflorescences, which are similar to other members of the Lamiaceae family. Salvia angustiarum is further classified within the genus Salvia, which includes over 900 species of flowering plants. The genus Salvia is often referred to as the sage genus, with many species being used for their medicinal and culinary properties. Within the genus Salvia, Salvia angustiarum is a specific species that is found in certain regions of the world.
benefits: Salvia angustiarum, commonly found in regions of Central and South America, has been known to possess several health benefits. Its leaves and stems contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Furthermore, Salvia angustiarum has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of stomach upset. Research also suggests that extracts from this plant may exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for certain infections. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and potential interactions.
parts: Salvia angustiarum is a species of the Salvia genus, but there isn't much information available about its specific medicinal parts. However, based on the general properties of the Salvia genus, the leaves and flowers are often used in herbal medicine. These parts may contain bioactive compounds that possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The leaves and flowers are typically harvested during the flowering stage, dried, and then used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions. More research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal properties of Salvia angustiarum and to identify its unique medicinal parts.
intro: Salvia angustiarum, also known as Narrow-leaved Sage, is a perennial herb belonging to the Salvia genus, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Salvia angustiarum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Salvia angustiarum is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed for their medicinal benefits, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine in the form of dried leaves and essential oils.
